name: compile
description: Translate a RiscvBtor2Spec into a BTOR2 reasoning artifact and return its content-addressed id. Determinism is guaranteed for a fixed (spec, source, schema_version) tuple. Run this after introspect confirms the spec is well-formed.

spec.description: A complete RiscvBtor2Spec object. Must satisfy the schema in gurdy/pairs/riscv_btor2/spec.py and validate_riscv_btor2_spec. Pair field must be "riscv-btor2".

name: dispatch
description: Run the named solver against a previously-compiled artifact and return its raw verdict. The directive picks the engine, bound, timeout, havoc set, and engine-specific options.

artifact_id.description: The id returned by a previous compile call.

directive.description: AnalysisDirective. Required: engine. Optional: bound (int), timeout (seconds, float), havoc_registers (array of register numbers 0..31), extra_options (object).

name: lift
description: Translate a raw solver result back to source-level steps. For reachable verdicts, returns a WitnessTrace with per-cycle (pc, mnemonic, regs); for proved verdicts (Pono / Spacer), returns a lifted invariant. Read off the witness JSON's bad_pc, anchor_step, and final_regs from the returned trace.

raw_result.description: A RawSolverResult object as returned by dispatch.

name: introspect
description: Run the pair's structural validator on a spec without compiling. Returns an array of Diagnostic objects (empty when the spec is well-formed). Use before compile to catch schema mismatches cheaply.
